items.title: Detecting and reducing scheming in AI models | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has published several notable research papers and announcements in early 2026. Key safety-focused research includes work on detecting and reducing scheming in AI models, evaluating chain-of-thought monitorability, and an analysis of AI progress with policy recommendations. On the applied science front, OpenAI released 'AI as a Scientific Collaborator' (January 2026), exploring how frontier models can accelerate scientific workflows including drug discovery and genomics. items.title: Introducing OpenAI o3 and o4-mini | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been actively releasing new models throughout early 2026. The most significant recent release is GPT-5.4, launched in March 2026, which features native computer-use capabilities allowing agents to operate computers and carry out complex workflows across applications. GPT-5.4 set records on benchmarks including OSWorld-Verified, WebArena Verified, and scored 83% on OpenAI's GDPval knowledge-work test. Alongside the flagship model, OpenAI released efficiency-focused variants: GPT-5.4 mi

items.title: OpenAI's existential questions | TechCrunch
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet:
OpenAI's most notable Twitter/X announcements in April 2026 center on Codex and subscription tier updates. OpenAI posted to @OpenAI announcing an update to ChatGPT Pro and Plus subscriptions to better support the growing use of Codex, introducing a new $100/month Pro tier that offers 5x more Codex usage than Plus, targeting longer and high-effort Codex sessions. This signals OpenAI's push to monetize developer-focused AI coding tools more aggressively.
